clusters.title: Pentagon identifies four of six US soldiers killed in Iran conflict
clusters.neutral_headline: US Soldiers Killed In Iran Conflict Identified

items.title: Pentagon identifies four of six US soldiers killed in Iran conflict
items.standfirst: The US soldiers were killed after a drone evaded air defences to hit a command centre in Port Shuaiba, Kuwait, on Sunday.
items.content: The US soldiers were killed after a drone evaded air defences to hit a command centre in Port Shuaiba, Kuwait, on Sunday.

items.title: What we know about U.S. service members killed in Iran war
items.standfirst: The fallen soldiers identified by the Pentagon were Sgt. Declan Coady, Sgt. 1st Class Nicole Amor, Capt. Cody Khork, and Sgt. 1st Class Noah Tietjens.
items.content: The fallen soldiers identified by the Pentagon were Sgt. Declan Coady, Sgt. 1st Class Nicole Amor, Capt. Cody Khork, and Sgt. 1st Class Noah Tietjens.

items.title: Families remember U.S. reservists killed in Kuwait, members of an Iowa logistics unit
items.standfirst: Four U.S. soldiers were killed in the Iran war on Sunday and IDed Tuesday by the Pentagon; two soldiers haven't yet been publicly identified. Their unit kept troops supplied with food and equipment.
items.content: Four U.S. soldiers were killed in the Iran war on Sunday and IDed Tuesday by the Pentagon; two soldiers haven't yet been publicly identified. Their unit kept troops supplied with food and equipment.(Image credit: Charlie Neibergall)

items.title: Pentagon releases names of first U.S. service members killed in Iran war
items.standfirst: Six American service members have been killed in the U.S.-Iran conflict, U.S. Central Command said.
items.content: Six American service members have been killed in the U.S.-Iran conflict, U.S. Central Command said.

items.title: What we know about U.S. service members killed in the Iran war
items.standfirst: Four of six U.S. service members killed in the Iran war died when a drone struck a port in Kuwait on Sunday, U.S.
items.content: Four of six U.S. service members killed in the Iran war died when a drone struck a port in Kuwait on Sunday, U.S.

ai_summary: Four US soldiers killed in Iran conflict identified
Soldiers were killed by drone strike in Kuwait
Pentagon releases names of killed soldiers
Six US service members killed in total
Two soldiers still not publicly identified

clusters.title: Spain accuses US of "playing Russian roulette" with millions of lives in Iran
clusters.neutral_headline: Spain Accuses US Of Playing Russian Roulette

items.title: Spain accuses US of "playing Russian roulette" with millions of lives in Iran
items.standfirst: Spain's Prime Minister doubled down on his criticism of Washington on March 3rd, a day after Donald Trump threatened to cut off all trade with the country. That itself came in response to Spain's refusal to allow the use of military bases shared with the US in its territory for Washington's continued war with Israel on Iran. Pedro Sanchez warned Trump he was playing Russian roulette with the lives of millions, and urged the world to learn from history.
items.content: Spain's Prime Minister doubled down on his criticism of Washington on March 3rd, a day after Donald Trump threatened to cut off all trade with the country. That itself came in response to Spain's refusal to allow the use of military bases shared with the US in its territory for Washington's continued war with Israel on Iran. Pedro Sanchez warned Trump he was playing Russian roulette with the lives of millions, and urged the world to learn from history.

items.title: Spain's Sanchez defies Trump trade threat, compares Iran war to ‘Russian roulette’
items.standfirst: Spain’s Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez doubled down Wednesday, criticising US and Israeli military actions in Iran and refusing to bow to trade pressure from Washington. In a defiant televised address, Sanchez warned that the escalating conflict in the Middle East was akin to “playing Russian roulette” with millions of lives.
items.content: Spain’s Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez doubled down Wednesday, criticising US and Israeli military actions in Iran and refusing to bow to trade pressure from Washington. In a defiant televised address, Sanchez warned that the escalating conflict in the Middle East was akin to “playing Russian roulette” with millions of lives.

items.title: ‘No to war’: Sánchez doubles down after Trump threat to cut off trade with Spain
items.standfirst: PM says his country will not be complicit in growing conflict in Middle East ‘simply out of fear of reprisals from someone’Middle East crisis – live updatesEurope live – latest updatesThe Spanish prime minister, Pedro Sánchez, has responded to Donald Trump’s extraordinary threat to cut off all trade with Spain over his government’s refusal to facilitate the US’s ongoing attacks against Iran, comparing the growing conflict in the Middle East to playing “Russian roulette with the destiny of millions”.Sánchez, who has been one of the most vociferous European critics of Israel’s conduct in Gaza, said his government’s position on the widening instability could be summed up in three words: “No to war.” Continue reading...
items.content: PM says his country will not be complicit in growing conflict in Middle East ‘simply out of fear of reprisals from someone’Middle East crisis – live updatesEurope live – latest updatesThe Spanish prime minister, Pedro Sánchez, has responded to Donald Trump’s extraordinary threat to cut off all trade with Spain over his government’s refusal to facilitate the US’s ongoing attacks against Iran, comparing the growing conflict in the Middle East to playing “Russian roulette with the destiny of millions”.Sánchez, who has been one of the most vociferous European critics of Israel’s conduct in Gaza, said his government’s position on the widening instability could be summed up in three words: “No to war.” Continue reading...

ai_summary: Spain criticizes US actions in Iran war
Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez speaks out
Spain refuses to allow US military bases
US threatens to cut off trade with Spain
Sanchez warns of Russian roulette with lives

clusters.title: Harry Maguire given 15-month suspended prison sentence after retrial in Greece

items.title: Harry Maguire given 15-month suspended prison sentence after retrial in Greece
items.standfirst: Player found guilty of ‘non-serious assault’ in 2020England defender to appeal again against verdictThe England and Manchester United defender Harry Maguire has been handed a 15-month suspended prison sentence by a Greek court over a 2020 incident in Mykonos.In 2020, Maguire was found guilty of repeated bodily harm, attempted bribery and violence against public employees after his arrest after a brawl outside a nightclub. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.content: Manchester United defender Harry Maguire has his suspended sentence reduced to 15 months at a retrial following an incident in Greece in 2020.

items.title: Why is Live Nation on trial? #entertainment
items.standfirst: Government alleges monopoly harms fans and venues Federal prosecutors have opened an antitrust case against Live Nation and its ticketing arm, accusing the company of using its market power to dominate the concert ticket ecosystem. The Justice Department says practices tied to Live Nation’s…
items.content: Government alleges monopoly harms fans and venues Federal prosecutors have opened an antitrust case against Live Nation and its ticketing arm, accusing the company of using its market power to dominate the concert ticket ecosystem. The Justice Department says practices tied to Live Nation’s…

items.title: ‘The concert ticket industry is broken,’ justice department says as Ticketmaster trial begins
items.standfirst: Entertainment giant keeps average of $7.58 of each ticket for events at major concert venues, court hearsSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxTicketmaster keeps an average of $7.58 of the price of each ticket for events at major concert venues, an attorney for New York state told jurors at a trial on Tuesday in which dozens of states are seeking to recoup damages for fans.Ticketmaster and its parent company, Live Nation, are accused of abusing their market power to prop up illegal monopolies in the concert industry. The trial in Manhattan could result in the US Department of Justice arguing for a breakup of Live Nation and Ticketmaster or the companies paying compensation to ticket purchasers. Continue reading...

The trial in Manhattan could result in the US Department of Justice arguing for a breakup of Live Nation and Ticketmaster or the companies paying compensation to ticket purchasers. Continue reading...
